question: Spain snap election called by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the next Spanish general election is scheduled between October 21, 2025, and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is about whether a date for the next Spanish election is announced within the stated timeframe. The date the election is scheduled to take place on will have no effect on the resolution to this market.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the Government of Spain however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Spain snap election called by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the next Spanish general election is scheduled between May 28 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is about whether a date for the next Spanish election is announced within the stated timeframe. The date the election is scheduled to take place on will have no effect on the resolution to this market.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the Government of Spain however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket for who calls a Spain snap election centers on Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez's minority PSOE government's fragility, bolstered recently by the October 2024 passage of a controversial amnesty law securing Catalan separatist Junts support despite protests and court challenges. Opposition PP leader Alberto Núñez Feijóo has demanded early elections amid PSOE trailing 15-20 points in polls, but Sánchez insists on serving the full term to 2027. No snap poll announced; trader odds reflect low near-term risk, pricing in budget negotiations as next catalyst—failure could trigger instability. Fragmented Congress dynamics and regional election precedents underscore uncertainty in coalition math.

question: French election called by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the date of the next French legislative election is declared by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For this market to resolve to "Yes" it is only necessary that the election date be declared, not that the election actually occur within the market timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the France, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: French election called by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the date of the next French parlimentary election is declared by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For this market to resolve to "Yes" it is only necessary that the election date be declared, not that the election actually occur within the market timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the France, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on whether and by whom a French election might be called centers on the persistent instability from President Macron's June 2024 snap dissolution of the National Assembly, yielding a fragmented hung parliament: New Popular Front at 182 seats, Ensemble centrists at 168, National Rally at 143, and others splitting the rest. No faction neared the 289-seat majority, fueling government crises—Michel Barnier's cabinet fell to a historic no-confidence vote on October 4, the first since 1962. Macron named ally François Bayrou prime minister on December 13 amid budget gridlock. Upcoming confidence votes and 2025 fiscal deadlines heighten risks of fresh dissolution, influencing market odds via trader assessments of deadlock resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Colorado Avalanche dominate trader sentiment at 78% implied probability for the Presidents' Trophy, driven by their league-leading 35-11-3 record and 73 points, a 10-point cushion over rivals with comparable games played. This market will resolve according to the player who is awarded the 2025–26 NHL Hart Memorial Trophy.

If the listed player is not announced as a finalist for the 2025–26 Hart Memorial Trophy, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NHL. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus crowns Nikita Kucherov and Nathan MacKinnon as Hart Trophy frontrunners at 53.3% and 42%, propelled by their scorching paces atop NHL scoring—Kucherov’s league-leading 46 points (15G-31A in 25GP) fueling Tampa Bay’s Atlantic surge (15-9-2), and MacKinnon’s 43 points (13G-30A in 26GP) powering Colorado’s Central dominance (17-8). Recent developments cement this: Kucherov’s eight multi-point outings in November, including four three-assisters, and MacKinnon’s streak amid Edmonton’s slump sidelining McDavid (4%). Rookie Macklin Celebrini (4.2%) shines for dismal San Jose but lacks team context; no injuries disrupt the duo’s MVP trajectories as playoffs loom.
